name: Western Rubble Line, Cantigny Village
description: The shattered western face of Cantigny — stone farmhouse walls reduced to waist-height, roof timbers collapsed across doorways, a cart overturned in what was once a village lane. The barrage has moved past here; this ground belongs to the Americans now, but it does not feel that way.
atmosphericDetails: Cordite smoke lies in the rubble at knee height, shifting with each concussion from the continuing barrage to the northeast
A German field-gray coat lies across a broken wall, its owner nowhere visible; a stick grenade with the pin still in it rests beside it
The sound here is layered — the distant thunder of the continuing barrage, the closer crack of small-arms fire in the village interior, and underneath it all, someone shouting in German from a cellar
